name: Mahogany Duck
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Fresh gingerroot, peeled and sliced
2 tsp Arrowroot dissolved in cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse ducks, pat dry, and remove excess fat from body cavities. Truss the birds.
2. Arrange them, breast side up, several inches apart on a rack set over a large roasting pan. Let them dry, chilled but uncovered, for 3 days.
3. In a bowl, combine Scotch whiskey, gingerroot, garlic, orange zest, coriander, peppercorns, soy sauce, honey, and brown sugar. Let the mixture stand, covered and chilled, for 3 days.
4. Stir marinade, press it through a fine sieve into a small bowl.
5. Keeping ducks chilled, brush them with some of the marinade every 30 minutes for 2-1/2 hours.
6. Let ducks dry at room temperature for 30 minutes.
7. Stuff each duck with one bread slice, 1 scallion, and 1 parsley sprig. Spoon remaining marinade into cavities.
8. Prick ducks (except breast area) with a fork.
9. Pour beer into the roasting pan. Roast ducks on the rack in the lower third of a preheated 350°F oven for 30 minutes.
10. Tent birds with foil and roast for another 30 minutes more. Discard foil and roast for another 30 minutes or until a meat thermometer registers 180°F. The skin should be very mahogany colored and crisp.
11. Remove stuffing ingredients with a spoon, discard them and pan juices. Pour juices from cavities through a fine sieve into a small bowl. Skim fat, reserve 1/4 cup of juices.
12. In saucepan, bring stock to boil, simmer for 15 minutes, then stir in reserved juices. Bring mixture to a simmer. Stir arrowroot mixture and add to pan. Cook over medium-low heat, not boiling, until thickened. Add salt and pepper to taste.
13. Transfer sauce to a heated serving dish. Garnish duck with kumquats and serve with sauce.
